items.title: The world’s largest privately owned laser just turned on
items.summary: Fusion startup Xcimer fired up the world's largest privately owned laser.

items.title: Squishmallows, dentures, and an ‘I Heart Hot Dads’ bag: Uber has found thousands of items left in robotaxis
items.summary: Even in a future of robot taxis, someone still has to return the things passengers leave behind.

items.title: Cyera eyes $12B valuation at 80x ARR multiple despite operating losses
items.summary: The cybersecurity company is nearing a $300 million round led by Evolution Equity Partners.

items.title: Cyberdecks are having a moment, rejecting big tech surveillance with style and substance
items.summary: Over the last few months, these DIY hardware communities have exploded in popularity as people on social media show off their solar-powered game emulators, pocket-sized ereaders, and clamshell purse computers.

items.title: Uber caps employee AI spending after blowing through budget in 4 months
items.summary: Uber's cutback has occurred after the company had reportedly encouraged staff to use AI as much as possible.

items.title: New Microsoft tool lets devs spin up AI behavior tests using text descriptions
items.summary: Microsoft on Tuesday took the wraps off Adaptive Spec-driven Scoring for Evaluation and Regression Testing, an open source framework for spinning up AI evaluations.

items.title: A startup, Everand, is now bundling e-books, audiobooks, and book clubs in challenge to Amazon
items.summary: A new reading subscription from Everand offers access to both e-books and audiobooks, and Fable's book club community.

items.title: Martin Scorsese becomes the latest — and most unlikely — Hollywood voice for AI
items.summary: The caveat is that one of the world's most famous living directors is using the tech solely for storyboarding.

items.title: Microsoft launches Scout, an OpenClaw-inspired personal assistant
items.summary: Launched at Build, Microsoft Scout is a new AI assistant meant to bring the power and flexibility of OpenClaw into the Microsoft 365 system.

items.title: Ex-Anduril engineer raises $42M to build the Amazon of composite parts
items.summary: Layup Parts co-founder Zack Eakin has drawn on a motorsports background, and his experience working for Palmer Luckey and Elon Musk, to tackle making faster, cheaper, and better composites.

items.title: Google rolls out fake call detection to protect against AI deepfake impersonation scams
items.summary: As people increasingly refuse to answer calls from unknown numbers, scammers are shifting their tactics by spoofing trusted phone numbers and using AI deepfake technology to sound like authority figures, family members, or employers.

items.title: Microsoft offers devs a better way to control AI agent behavior
items.summary: The specification lets developer, compliance, and security teams define their own policies for agents to follow in portable policy files.

items.title: Amazon faces class action lawsuit over Ring facial-recognition feature
items.summary: The class action lawsuit, filed in Seattle by Virginia resident Charles Sigwalt, claims that Ring's Familiar Faces feature stores images of passersby without consent.

items.title: Focused Energy raises whopping $240M Series A for laser-powered fusion tech
items.summary: Another fusion startup has raised another a massive round to make this type of power a reality.

items.title: Trump signs narrower executive order on AI oversight after industry objections
items.summary: After industry objections, President Trump signed a revised AI executive order requiring only voluntary prerelease government reviews of advanced models.

items.title: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
items.summary: OpenAI released a set of six plug-ins aimed at specific jobs: data analytics, creative production, sales, product design, equity investing, and investment banking. Available from within the Codex app, each of the new tools bundles integrations, instructions, and context to allow Codex to approximate a specific job.

items.title: Board, the new game startup from Mirror founder Brynn Putnam, raises $20M, has already sold thousands
items.summary: Board, the startup building what it calls "together tech" designed to bring people into the same room, has closed a Series A led by Union Square Ventures.

items.title: Password manager Dashlane says hackers stole some customers’ password vaults
items.summary: The password manager giant said hackers were able to "brute-force" its two-factor system, allowing them to access customer accounts and download their password vaults.

items.title: Why VivaTech 2026 is the place to see Europe’s AI strategy take shape
items.summary: TechCrunch is partnering with VivaTech 2026 to spotlight some of the most important conversations shaping the future of artificial intelligence. Join us in Paris June 17-20!

items.title: Meta tests ‘Series’ for episodic Reels on Instagram and Facebook
items.summary: Meta told TechCrunch that it's considering ways to monetize the new feature, but didn't share specifics on what that could look like.
